name: Referee Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. In a large bowl, combine brown sugar, shortening, water, and vanilla. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Add eggs and beat into the creamed mixture.
3. Sift together flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt. Mix into the creamed mixture at low speed just until blended. Stir in chocolate chips.
4. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls (15 mL) of dough 2 inches (5 cm) apart onto ungreased baking sheets.
5. Bake one baking sheet at a time at 375°F (190°C) for 7 to 9 minutes, or until cookies are set. Do not overbake. Allow cookies to cool for 2 minutes on the baking sheet before removing them to the foil to cool completely.
6. For icing, place white chocolate chips and shortening in a heavy resealable sandwich bag. Seal. Microwave at 50% (MEDIUM) power. Knead the bag after 1 minute. Repeat until smooth or melt by placing the bag in a bowl of hot water. Cut a tiny tip off the corner of the bag. Pipe thin stripes on cookies.
Makes: About 3 dozen cookies.
